close_reason: Already exists: BACKEND const was already defined
description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs has the literal `"keyring"` repeated at lines 68, 70, 119, 126, 130, 137, 161, 166, 170, 177, 184, 194 — 12 occurrences across the file. A `const BACKEND: &str = "keyring";` at the top of the file would centralize this and make rename refactors safe. The sibling crate secretx-wolfhsm uses exactly this pattern (secretx-wolfhsm/src/lib.rs has `const BACKEND: &str = "wolfhsm";` referenced via `backend: BACKEND`).

Suggested rewrite:
```rust
const BACKEND: &str = "keyring";
```
Then replace every `backend: "keyring"` with `backend: BACKEND` and every `expected backend \`keyring\`` template with `format!("expected backend \...")` or similar.

This is bikeshedding-tier on its own but combined with a future rename (e.g. if the URI scheme were ever changed) it is a real maintenance benefit.
title: Backend name "keyring" repeated as string literal 8+ times — extract to const
